justification: All pass criteria met: (1) Authentication per consumer type designed correctly — JWT for mobile via OAuth 2.0+PKCE, API key for B2B partners, with detailed validation flows. (2) Internal traffic separation fully addressed — dedicated Internal Gateway/mesh with mTLS, network-level isolation, explicit rejection of external gateway traffic for internal services. (3) Business logic explicitly excluded from gateway — stated multiple times including in boundaries, pitfalls_to_avoid, and trade_offs. (4) SPOF risk identified as critical severity with multi-AZ active-active mitigation. (5) Webhook delivery for partners addressed comprehensively — dedicated Webhook Dispatcher component using transactional outbox pattern with exponential backoff retry. (6) flexibility_points (5) and rigidity_points (3) present and well-reasoned. (7) Technology recommendation is gateway-product-agnostic with explicit build/buy consideration — mentions Kong, AWS API Gateway, Envoy, nginx, and frames the choice around team context rather than prescribing a single product.

observations.text: Exceptionally thorough separation of internal vs external traffic with network-level isolation, not just logical separation
Business logic exclusion from gateway is reinforced in multiple sections — component boundaries, trade_offs, and pitfalls_to_avoid
Webhook delivery design goes beyond basic requirements with transactional outbox, exponential backoff, dead-letter queue, and per-partner circuit breakers
Build vs buy addressed well — technology-agnostic architecture with explicit note that choice depends on team infrastructure context
Fail-open rate limiting with Redis is a nuanced design decision with clear trade-off analysis
Strangler fig migration pattern with phased implementation guidance is practical and reduces risk
Gateway misconfiguration risk explicitly called out as critical severity with defense-in-depth mitigation
Auth vs authz separation correctly designed — gateway handles identity, services handle authorization

agent_output_excerpt: {"executive_summary":"Design a unified API Gateway architecture for a logistics platform serving three distinct consumer types (mobile, B2B partner, internal). The gateway centralizes authentication, rate limiting, and traffic isolation—replacing the current inconsistent per-service implementations. The architecture uses a single gateway deployment with three logical ingress lanes, each applying consumer-specific policies, fronting a shared backend service mesh isolated via network segmentation.
